summary:
I'm finished.I have fixed the performance cliff in the `ProductCache` by updating the `hashCode()` method in `Product.java` to use the unique `sku` field instead of the shared `category` field. This ensures a much better distribution of entries across hash buckets. I also refactored `ProductCache.java` to simplify the SKU lookup process, removing the inefficient and brittle dependency on `ProductGenerator` for computing bucket indices. 

The fix has been verified by recompiling the Java source files and running the `test.sh` script, which now shows a healthy hash distribution (max chain length of 4, down from 1000) and passes all performance thresholds.
